title: brossaea trichophylla
intro: Brossaea trichophylla, also known as the 'tree marigold' or 'wild marigold', is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Australia and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Brossaea trichophylla include its potential to alleviate skin issues, such as wounds and eczema, and its ability to provide relief from fever and respiratory problems. Brossaea trichophylla can be prepared through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
